name: Aldeire, río Benéjar y el Castillo de La Calahorra — circular por el Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada
region_intro: Mountain biking around Aldeire offers access to the diverse and challenging terrain of the Sierra Nevada National Park. The region is characterized by mountainous landscapes, including rocky tracks, dirt trails, and sections with loose stones, ideal for varied riding experiences. Riders can explore picturesque oak and chestnut forests, as well as valleys carved by the Benéjar and Benabre rivers. High-altitude riding is accessible, with the village itself at 1297 meters and nearby mountain passes like Puerto de La Ragua. Best mountain bike trails around Aldeire The most popular mountain bike trail is Transnevada Route 4/8 - walk through the Sierra Nevada National Park, a 68.7 miles (110.6 km) trail that takes 11 hours 17 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses significant elevation changes within the national park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is El Horcajo — circular desde Aldeire — Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada, a moderate 16.5 miles (26.6 km) path. This route offers a circular journey through the Sierra Nevada National Park, showcasing its natural beauty. Local mountain bikers also love the Transnevada Trail: Puerto de la Ragua - Stage 5 Loop, a 15.1 miles (24.3 km) trail leading through the high-altitude Puerto de la Ragua area, often completed in about 2 hours 16 minutes. Mountain biking around Aldeire is defined by the rugged terrain of the Sierra Nevada National Park, dense oak and chestnut forests, and high mountain passes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Aldeire are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Aldeire's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Aldeire?
A: Aldeire offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 35 routes available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 2 easy, 21 moderate, and 12 difficult trails, ensuring options for every rider.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Aldeire?
A: The terrain around Aldeire is diverse and challenging, characteristic of the Sierra Nevada National Park. You can expect rocky tracks, dirt trails, and sections with loose stones. Many routes feature significant elevation changes, with challenging climbs and thrilling descents through picturesque oak and chestnut forests, and along valleys carved by the Benéjar and Benabre rivers.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Aldeire?
A: Spring and fall generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Aldeire, with mild temperatures. While summer can be warmer at lower altitudes, it's ideal for higher elevation climbs. The region's high altitude, with Aldeire itself at 1297 meters, means conditions can vary, so checking local weather is always recommended.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Aldeire?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017314950/el-horcajo-circular-desde-aldeire-parque-nacional-de-sierra-nevada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Horcajo — circular desde Aldeire — Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic journey through the Sierra Nevada National Park. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185527689/aldeire-rio-benejar-y-el-castillo-de-la-calahorra-circular-por-el-parque-nacional-de-sierra-nevada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldeire, río Benéjar y el Castillo de La Calahorra — circular por el Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada&lt;/a&gt;, which combines natural beauty with historical sights.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking around Aldeire?
A: Mountain biking around Aldeire offers access to stunning natural features within the Sierra Nevada National Park. You can encounter the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3866790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benéjar River&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4418355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Rivers (Cruce de Los Ríos) – El Horcajo&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5412222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Benéjar River in El Horcajo&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also known for its picturesque oak and chestnut forests.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers historical and cultural attractions. The 11th-century medieval fortress, Castillo de la Caba, is perched above Aldeire. You can also explore the nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5060351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadix&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique cave dwellings, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4852722" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadix Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6590435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Theatre of Guadix&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1185527689/aldeire-rio-benejar-y-el-castillo-de-la-calahorra-circular-por-el-parque-nacional-de-sierra-nevada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldeire, río Benéjar y el Castillo de La Calahorra&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate these points of interest.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Aldeire?
A: While many trails in Aldeire are moderate to difficult due to the mountainous terrain, there are 2 easy routes available that might be suitable for families or beginners. It's always recommended to check the specific route details and elevation profiles to ensure they match your family's skill level and endurance.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Aldeire?
A: The mountain bike trails around Aldeire are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.7 out of 5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs, thrilling descents, and the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra Nevada National Park.

Q: Are there any permits or regulations for mountain biking in the Sierra Nevada National Park?
A: As Aldeire is located within the Sierra Nevada National Park, it's advisable to be aware of any specific regulations or permits that might apply to mountain biking within protected areas. While general access is often permitted on established trails, certain zones or activities might require prior authorization. It's best to consult the official Sierra Nevada National Park website for the most current information.

Q: Where can I find amenities like cafes or restaurants near the trails in Aldeire?
A: Aldeire, as a village, offers some local amenities. Additionally, recreational areas like La Rosandrá, located along the Benéjar river, are ideal for picnics and serve as starting points for outdoor activities. For a wider range of options, nearby towns or larger settlements within the region would provide more choices for cafes, pubs, restaurants, and accommodation.

Q: Can I bring my dog on mountain bike trails in Aldeire?
A: While the Sierra Nevada National Park is a natural environment, specific regulations regarding dogs on trails can vary. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check for specific signage or park guidelines on individual trails or within certain protected zones to confirm dog-friendly policies.

Q: What are the options for parking or public transport for mountain biking in Aldeire?
A: Aldeire is a small village, so parking might be available in or around the village center, or at designated trailheads. For public transport, options might be limited, with bus services connecting to larger towns. It's advisable to research specific public transport routes and schedules if you plan to rely on them, or to consider driving and parking at a suitable starting point for your chosen route.
tips: Small town in the southern part of the Guadix region, Granada, located at an altitude of 1,297 meters. Archaeological sites indicate that the area has been populated since prehistoric times. The origin of the current town dates from the Arab period, just like its name, which comes from the Arabic term "al-Dar", in Spanish "the house".
Nice path with a moderate slope that allows us to enjoy the small waterfalls of the Benéjar river.
Place where the Benéjar river is born, perfect for taking a break to enjoy the landscape and, in very hot seasons, take the opportunity to cool off.

Perched on a hill that visually dominates the Marquesado del Cenete, at 1,250 meters, the Castle of La Calahorra is a spectacular monument of Renaissance construction.

This stone mass was raised in a very short space of time on the remains of the Arab fortress that was previously established in the same place.
